title: AI Reshaping News and Storytelling

modules.title: Automated Reporting: Bots Writing News
modules.sub: How algorithms generate news articles without human writers
modules.description: Learners examine how natural language generation systems produce news at scale, exploring real examples from financial and sports reporting. They assess the strengths and limitations of bot-authored content and decide where automation adds value versus where it falls short.

modules.title: AI Tools in Modern Newsrooms
modules.sub: Surveying the AI systems journalists use every day
modules.description: Learners map the AI-powered tools embedded in contemporary newsroom workflows, from transcription and translation to image tagging and story suggestion engines. They evaluate how these tools affect journalist workload, editorial judgment, and the overall quality of reporting.

modules.title: Personalization Algorithms and News Bubbles
modules.sub: How recommendation engines shape what audiences actually see
modules.description: Learners investigate how platform algorithms curate individualized news feeds and the filter bubbles that result. They analyze case studies of algorithmic amplification and consider the civic consequences of audiences receiving fundamentally different versions of reality.

modules.title: Synthetic Media and Editorial Responsibility
modules.sub: Navigating deepfakes, AI images, and fabricated audio in journalism
modules.description: Learners study how synthetic media is created and how it enters the news ecosystem, often undetected. They work through editorial scenarios to determine what verification standards and disclosure obligations responsible outlets should adopt when AI-generated content is involved.

modules.title: Fact-Checking at Machine Speed
modules.sub: Using AI to verify claims faster without sacrificing accuracy
modules.description: Learners explore AI-assisted fact-checking pipelines and the tools newsrooms use to detect misinformation at scale. They critically assess where automated verification succeeds, where human judgment remains irreplaceable, and how to design hybrid workflows that maintain credibility.

modules.title: Audience Manipulation and Influence Campaigns
modules.sub: Identifying AI-driven tactics used to mislead and polarize audiences
modules.description: Learners dissect coordinated influence operations that use AI to generate disinformation, fabricate personas, and micro-target susceptible audiences. They develop frameworks for recognizing manipulation signatures and evaluate the responsibilities of platforms, journalists, and regulators in response.

modules.title: Ethics of AI-Assisted Journalism
modules.sub: Applying ethical principles when AI shares the editorial role
modules.description: Learners engage with core ethical frameworks — transparency, fairness, accountability, and harm reduction — and apply them to contested decisions in AI-assisted journalism. They work through dilemmas involving algorithmic bias, source privacy, and the boundaries of acceptable automation in the newsroom.

modules.title: Designing Trustworthy AI-Powered Media
modules.sub: Building media products audiences can understand and trust
modules.description: Learners synthesize the course by designing guidelines or product principles for a trustworthy AI-powered media organization. They make concrete decisions about transparency labels, human oversight checkpoints, and accountability structures, producing a practical framework they can apply in their own professional context.

synopsis: Artificial intelligence is changing who creates the news, how stories are told, and which audiences receive which information — raising urgent questions about truth, trust, and editorial accountability. This course unpacks the AI systems behind modern media production and gives learners the tools to critically evaluate the news they consume.
rationale: With AI now generating news articles, personalizing content feeds, and enabling sophisticated influence operations, journalism literacy and AI literacy have become inseparable skills for an informed public.
